jobs.feedName: David Nazarian College of Business & Economics
jobs.title: CHC BOLD PAC Internship at CHC BOLD PAC
jobs.description: Employer: CHC BOLD PAC Expires: 07/31/2026 Are you looking for an opportunity to break into politics? Want to establish lasting connections and gain campaign experience? Would you like to work with Members of the Congressional Hispanic Caucus? CHC BOLD PAC’s Tony Cárdenas Internship in Politics Program is designed to give individuals substantial exposure to politics on a national level by helping to build and grow the political arm of the Democratic Hispanic Members in the House and Senate. CHC BOLD PAC is dedicated to increasing the diversity in the House and Senate by supporting Latino and Latina candidates running for federal office across the country. BOLD PAC is made up of 42 Members of the U.S. House and Senate. BOLD PAC is also committed to increasing opportunities for Latino and Latina young professionals in Democratic politics, and is excited to sponsor an internship to help bridge the gap. Who are we looking for? Smart, energetic, and fun individuals who are interested in gaining experience and involvement in politics.Individuals with an interest in supporting and electing more Latinos and Latinas to the U.S. House and Senate.Individuals interested in learning how to work with media firms to create digital content.What are the benefits of the CHC BOLD PAC Internship Program? An unprecedented opportunity to work day-to-day with Members of Congress, individuals on their senior staff, and political operatives.Receive first-hand experience with fundraising for a Political Action Committee. The opportunity to network with leaders in the field. The Tony Cárdenas Internship in Politics is full-time and based in Washington, DC. The start date is Wednesday, September 2, 2026 and the end date is Friday, December 11, 2026. Start and end dates are flexible, especially around academic calendars. This is an internship with a maximum stipend of $1,200 a month, based on the length of participation in the program. Part-time internships may also be available. PROGRAM DETAILS:Duration: September 2026 - December 2026Hours: Full timeCompensation: $1,200/month stipend plus $50/month transportation stipendLocation: Washington, DC and HybridHOW TO APPLY:Applications must be submitted through the Google Form, available here. If you have any questions, please email robert@chcboldpac.com.The application deadline is Sunday, July 26, 2026 at 11PM ESTEQUAL OPPORTUNITY/DIVERSITY POLICY CHC BOLD PAC prohibits discrimination of employment, promotion, compensation, terms, conditions, or privileges of employment on the basis of gender, disability, race, age, national origin, color, creed, sexual orientation, sex (including marital and parental status), gender identity and expression, religion, economic status, ethnic identity, veteran's status, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law. This policy covers all programs, services, policies, and procedures of CHC BOLD PAC, including opportunity for employment and treatment as a CHC BOLD PAC employee. CHC BOLD PAC is an equal opportunity employer and will comply with all applicable laws prohibiting discrimination in employment. CHC BOLD PAC’s equal opportunity policy covers all programs, services, policies, and procedures of the CHC BOLD PAC, including opportunity for employment and treatment as a DCCC employee, as well as opportunities for Consultants to contract with the CHC BOLD PAC. CHC BOLD PAC is committed to building a staff that reflects the diverse communities that makeup our country and the Democratic Party. Working towards the goal of a diverse CHC BOLD PAC our policy extends to both Staff and Consultants.

jobs.feedName: David Nazarian College of Business & Economics
jobs.title: Fund Operations Intern - Fall 2026 at Fund Evaluation Group LLC
jobs.description: Employer: Fund Evaluation Group LLC Expires: 07/31/2026 Intern, Curio Fund Operations Location: Cincinnati, OH (On-site)Employment Type: Full-time (40 hours / week)Department: FEG Curio Investment Partners About the Role / Position SummaryThe Fund Operations Intern supports day-to-day fund operations, investor support, client deliverables, documentation, and project management activities across FEG Curio Investment Partners. This role is designed to assist the Fund Operations team with a focus on supporting responsibilities related to Curio Select Investors, fund administration workflows, investor onboarding, and operational process improvement. Why Work at FEG? / About the CompanyFEG is an independently owned advisory firm that provides investment consulting, outsourced chief investment officer (OCIO), and research services to predominantly institutional clients1, such as university endowments, private and public foundations, religious organizations, healthcare institutions, corporate retirement plans, and select family offices. Founded in 1988, FEG has served communities both directly and by helping our clients fulfill their missions, for over 37 years. FEG employs 140+ professionals across offices in Cincinnati, Ohio, Dallas, Texas, and Indianapolis, Indiana, with approximately $99.4 billion2 in client assets under advisement. What You’ll Do / Position ResponsibilitiesFund Operations, Client, and Reporting SupportSupport the Fund Operations team with recurring administrative, documentation, and reporting workflows related to Curio Select Investors, including:Assist with investor subscription, AML/KYC, onboarding, and related documentationHelp organize investor files, subscription materials, approvals, notices, reconciliations, and supporting recordsTrack operational items, deadlines, follow-ups, and deliverables across internal teams, fund administrators, and third-party service providersSupport preparation of client, investor, administrator, and meeting materialsHelp organize financial reporting materials, including capital account statements, valuation materials, manager reporting, NAV workpapers, and reconciliation summariesMaintain workflow trackers, checklists, shared files, SharePoint folders, and other documentation repositories FEG Managed Portfolios and Project SupportAssist with FEG Managed Portfolios, team projects, and process improvement initiatives, including:Support updates to FEG Managed Portfolios materials, model information, and related implementation filesHelp prepare monthly performance and risk updates, portfolio analytics, sales team talking points, and internal presentation materialsAssist with data maintenance for DDQs, RFIs, fact sheets, marketing collateral, and other advisor-facing materialsHelp gather and organize supporting documentation for GIPS, composite, and compliance review processesMaintain project lists, task trackers, deliverable calendars, and recurring workflow documentationCoordinate with internal teams to support timely completion of recurring deliverables and process improvement projects What We Require / Minimum QualificationsCurrent undergraduate student pursuing a degree in finance, accounting, economics, business, operations, data analytics, or a related fieldStrong interest in financial services, investment operations, private funds, or alternative investmentsStrong attention to detail and organizational skillsProficiency with Microsoft Office, especially Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and OutlookAbility to handle confidential information responsiblyAvailability to work 40 hours / week during the 2026 Fall Semester (August 2026 – December 2026) What You’ll Bring / Knowledge, Skills, and ExperienceStrong written and verbal communication skillsAbility to manage deadlines, organize information, and follow through on assigned tasksComfort working with spreadsheets, shared folders, workflow trackers, and documentation systemsWillingness to ask questions, learn new processes, and take ownership of assigned responsibilitiesCollaborative mindset and ability to work with investment, operations, compliance, client service, and administrative teamsCuriosity about alternative investments, private funds, investor onboarding, and fund administrationHigh level of discretion, professionalism, and accuracy Work Authorization RequirementFEG Investment Advisors is committed to fostering diversity and maintaining equitable, inclusive hiring practices. At this time, we are not able to provide visa sponsorship for this role. All candidates must already have legal authorization to work in the United States at the time they apply and remain authorized throughout their employment, without requiring current or future sponsorship. LIMITATIONS AND DISCLAIMERThe above job description is meant to describe the general nature and level of work being performed; it is not intended to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties and skills required for the position. All job requirements are subject to possible modification to reasonably accommodate individuals with disabilities. Some requirements may exclude individuals who pose a direct threat or significant risk to the health and safety of themselves or other employees. This job description in no way states or implies that these are the only duties to be performed by the employee occupying this position. Employees will be required to follow any other job-related instructions and to perform other job-related duties requested by their supervisor in compliance with Federal and State Laws. Requirements are representative of minimum levels of knowledge, skills and/or abilities. To perform this job successfully, the employee must possess the abilities or aptitudes to perform each duty proficiently. Continued employment remains on an “at-will” basis. 1Institutional clients include Charitable organizations, Community Foundations, Corporate, Healthcare, Higher Education, Independent Schools, Insurance, Native American Tribes, Private Foundations, Public Funds, Religious organizations, and Taft Hartley. Nonprofit clients include Charitable organizations, Community Foundations, Healthcare (nonprofit), Higher Education, Independent Schools, Private Foundations, and Religious Organizations. 2As of December 31, 2025, Assets under Advisement (AUA) include discretionary and non-discretionary assets of FEG and its affiliated entities. These assets are typically non-discretionary. Some asset values may not be readily available at the most recent quarter-end; therefore, the previous quarter's values were used and may be higher or lower depending on current market conditions. Of the $99.4bn in AUA, FEG's total assets under management (AUM) of $18.1bn includes discretionary of ($15.4bn) and non-discretionary AUM ($2.6bn). FEG Investment Advisors is an Equal Opportunity Employer. FEG Investment Advisors does not discriminate in its employment decisions based on race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity and sexual orientation), age (40 or older), disability, veteran or military status, genetic information, or any other basis that would violate any applicable federal, state, or local law.

jobs.feedName: David Nazarian College of Business & Economics
jobs.title: 2027 Wealth Management Intern at The Lighthouse Wealth Management Group at Morgan Stanley
jobs.description: Employer: The Lighthouse Wealth Management Group at Morgan Stanley Expires: 07/31/2026 To apply email your resume to Benjamin.Scott1@morganstanley.com Wealth Management Internship- Spring 2027 Rotational internship program with the Lighthouse Wealth Management Group at Morgan Stanley, in person, in Ponte Vedra Beach, FL. Starting January 2027. All hours are required to be completed, in the office- no remote work. Pay is $20/ hour 10 hours a week minimum- 20 hours a week maximum. Schedule is flexible and will be determined after the candidate is selected to accommodate their academic schedule. 15 week rotational program. Intern will spend 4 weeks in each rotation: Financial Advisor Analyst Client Service AssociateThe final weeks will be spent working on a “capstone” project, which will be presented to the team.The program is meant to be a “try-out” for a full time role as either an analyst or a client service associate. Looking for a spring intern who is graduating and looking to start a full time position in the Summer of 2027. EDUCATION, EXPERIENCE, KNOWLEDGE and SKILLS:Education and/or Experience• Successful completion of the SIE exam before 01/2027• On track to complete a Four-year college degree no later than 06/2027• Previous wealth management, banking, or finance internship experience preferred Knowledge/Skills• Knowledge of financial services products, including but not limited to equities, bonds, options, mutual funds, annuities, insurance, and managed accounts• Technically proficient and quick learner of new and updated platforms• Detail oriented with superior organizational skills and ability to prioritize• Expert knowledge of basic personal computer, MS Office (particularly MS Excel) and internet applications• Exceptional writing, interpersonal and client service skills• Strong time management skills• Team player with the ability to collaborate with others• Ability to work in a fast-paced, evolving environment• Adaptable and ability to multi-task• Goal oriented, self-motivated and results driven Reports to:• Business Service Officer WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM MORGAN STANLEY: At Morgan Stanley, we raise, manage and allocate capital for our clients – helping them reach their goals. We do it in a way that’s differentiated – and we’ve done that for 90 years. Our values - putting clients first, doing the right thing, leading with exceptional ideas, committing to diversity and inclusion, and giving back - aren’t just beliefs, they guide the decisions we make every day to do what's best for our clients, communities and more than 80,000 employees in 1,200 offices across 42 countries. At Morgan Stanley, you’ll find an opportunity to work alongside the best and the brightest, in an environment where you are supported and empowered. Our teams are relentless collaborators and creative thinkers, fueled by their diverse backgrounds and experiences. We are proud to support our employees and their families at every point along their work-life journey, offering some of the most attractive and comprehensive employee benefits and perks in the industry. There’s also ample opportunity to move about the business for those who show passion and grit in their work. To learn more about our offices across the globe, please copy and paste https://www.morganstanley.com/about-us/global-offices​ into your browser. Expected base pay rates for the role will be between $68,000 and $120,000 per year at the commencement of employment. However, base pay if hired will be determined on an individualized basis and is only part of the total compensation package, which, depending on the position, may also include commission earnings, incentive compensation, discretionary bonuses, other short and long-term incentive packages, and other Morgan Stanley sponsored benefit programs. Morgan Stanley's goal is to build and maintain a workforce that is diverse in experience and background but uniform in reflecting our standards of integrity and excellence. Consequently, our recruiting efforts reflect our desire to attract and retain the best and brightest from all talent pools. We want to be the first choice for prospective employees. It is the policy of the Firm to ensure equal employment opportunity without discrimination or harassment on the basis of race, color, religion, creed, age, sex, sex stereotype, gender, gender identity or expression, transgender, sexual orientation, national origin, citizenship, disability, marital and civil partnership/union status, pregnancy, veteran or military service status, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by law. Morgan Stanley is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversifying its workforce (M/F/Disability/Vet).

jobs.feedName: David Nazarian College of Business & Economics
jobs.title: Senior Quality Management (QCO) Intern - Graduate Students at Massachusetts Bay Transportation Authority
jobs.description: Employer: Massachusetts Bay Transportation Authority Expires: 07/31/2026 Quality Compliance and Oversight (QCO) vision is to support the MBTA in building a culture of excellence through proactive collaboration, dependable outcomes, and a commitment to continuous improvement. Guided by a human-centered approach, QCO prioritizes listening to align with stakeholder needs, facilitating meaningful connections, and meeting teams where they are. The team learns from frontline experiences, communicates openly, and focuses on solving what matters most to support successes and drive lasting, sustainable change. The Senior Intern will be working alongside the Senior Director of Quality Management (QM) situated within the QCO Department. This role will contribute and provide support for the operations, planning and logistics needed to support QM and assistance with special projects. The Senior Intern is for someone who is looking to develop and strengthen business and process improvement skills while working on QCO-related cross-functional projects. Your need to have ability to successfully assist MBTA staff in executing on process improvement will require strong communication skills, patience, and a solution-oriented attitude. This internship provides a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience while supporting one of the largest transit systems in the nation. You will get guidance and mentorship from the MBTA’s Quality, Compliance and Oversight team. Working in QCO, you will be on the front line of a mission critical engagement essential to the operation and reputation of the MBTA. The MBTA offers internship start dates on the first Monday of September, December, March, and June. We’re looking for candidates who can commit to six months’ experience, allowing for a more meaningful and impactful contribution. We are looking to hire 1 intern, who will work at least 2 - 3 days onsite (40 hours per week) for at least six months (September – February). Principal Duties and Responsibilities This role will contribute and provide support for the operations, planning and logistics needed to support the QM team and assistance with special projects. Support Quality Management activities related to the Audit Workstream and Corrective Action Plan (CAP) Workstreams, including assisting with tracking deliverables, organizing documentation, monitoring action items, preparing meeting materials, and coordinating follow-up with internal stakeholders to support timely completion and continuous improvement.Assist the Sr Director of QM and others with their duties related to the development, implementation, and close-out of regulatory findings from oversight authorities such as Federal Transit Agency (FTA) and the Department of Public Utilities (DPU), and/or enterprise projects that further the MBTA’s strategic goals or to address deficiencies and/or opportunities for improvement at the MBTA.Participate in team / staff meetings, organization events, fieldwork, project teams, or other duties as needed.Additional responsibilities may include focusing on one or more departments or locations. See applicable addendums or hiring manager for department or location specific functions. Provide internal and external customers with courteous and professional experience.Perform all other duties and projects that may be assigned.* The statements contained in this job description are intended to describe the general nature and complexity of the work being performed by staff assigned to this job and do not represent an exhaustive list of all duties, tasks, and responsibilities required of staff assigned to this position. The physical demands and work environment characteristics described here-in are representative of those an employee may encounter while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations can be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform essential functions. Minimum Education Enrolled, for entire duration of employment, in the graduate / master’s degree program of an accredited college / university. Minimum Experience and Required SkillsAbove average proficiency in project management.Above average knowledge of Microsoft Office suite (Office 365 preferred).At least one year of professional experience in Quality Management.Above average data analysis skills.Above average analytical, conceptual, and problem-solving abilities.Above average ability to communicate clearly and concisely, presenting ideas in user-friendly language.Above average presentation and interpersonal skills.Above average ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with peers.Above average ability to conform to shifting priorities, demands, and timelines.Above average ability to manage multiple tasks with minimal supervision.Above average ability to communicate effectively in English, both in written and oral forms.Highly organized, motivated, and have great attention-to-detail.Strong interest in public service, transit, or transportation and how it operates.Above average ability to work effectively in diverse, collaborative, fast-paced environments as well as independently.Ability to independently commute to assigned work locations in the Boston, MA metro area, as required by the role.Authorized to work in the United States (international students in CPT, STEM, or OPT programs through a university are eligible to apply). The MBTA does not provide visa sponsorship, such as H1-B status.* The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and complexity of the work being performed by staff assigned to this job and do not represent an exhaustive list of all duties, tasks, and responsibilities required.

jobs.title: Summer 2027 Internship at Ace Electric, Inc.
jobs.description: Employer: Ace Electric, Inc. jobs.description: Employer: Thrive Hair Bar Expires: 07/31/2026 Thrive Hair Bar is looking for its Summer marketing intern!Each season we have the opportunity to work with creative students who have great ideas for how to best market services, products, and experiences. These students have great attention to detail, learn & grow from their experiences, and thrive on constructive feedback. Is Thrive Hair Bar's next great marketing intern you?We're looking for someone who is passionate about disrupting the American beauty standard, who is passionate about helping people feel comfortable, confident, and empowered in their true selves, and who will be comfortable with constantly adapting to a rapidly evolving work environment by helping Thrive Hair Bar realize it's marketing strategy that includes live, in-person events, social media (Instagram & Facebook), virtual events, classes, and frequent collaborations with industry leaders and medical professionals.About Thrive Hair BarThrive Hair Bar DC is a premier, luxury natural hair salon proudly located in one of Washington, DC’s most vibrant and historic neighborhoods, H Street NE. We specialize in natural hair styling services, including twists, locs, braids, and blowouts, tailored to enhance and celebrate the beauty of kinky, coily, and curly hair. In addition to our expert styling, we offer personalized consultations and a curated selection of retail products to empower our clients to embrace and care for their unique hair journey. Committed to fostering a sense of community and empowerment, we also host local events that celebrate diversity and promote the normalization of natural hair in every facet of life, from professional to personal to luxurious. At Thrive Hair Bar, we believe in the transformative power of embracing one’s natural beauty, and we are dedicated to helping our clients feel confident, empowered and utterly fabulous in their natural glory.Internship ResponsibilitiesThis role expectations are that you dedicate at least 8 hours per week to Thrive Hair Bar assignments which are due 1x per week, attend weekly one on ones with the CEO, and work in person at least 1x per week for a minimum of 2 hours. jobs.feedName: David Nazarian College of Business & Economics
jobs.title: Senior Records & Information Management (QCO) Intern - Graduate Students at Massachusetts Bay Transportation Authority
jobs.description: Employer: Massachusetts Bay Transportation Authority Expires: 07/31/2026 Quality Compliance and Oversight (QCO) vision is to support the MBTA in building a culture of excellence through proactive collaboration, dependable outcomes, and a commitment to continuous improvement. Guided by a human-centered approach, QCO prioritizes listening to align with stakeholder needs, facilitating meaningful connections, and meeting teams where they are. The team learns from frontline experiences, communicates openly, and focuses on solving what matters most to support successes and drive lasting, sustainable change. The Senior Intern will be working alongside Records and Information Management staff (aka RIM, situated within the Document Management (DM) group in the Office of Quality, Compliance, and Oversight) on a variety of important Records and Information Management projects. The RIM Intern will contribute to DM’s overarching objective to implement systems and standards in support of the MBTA that enhance the quality of information, guaranteeing its integrity and accessibility, for the lifecycle of the records through the application of Document Control, Records and Information Management, and Archiving best practices. This internship provides a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in records and information management while supporting one of the largest transit systems in the nation. The MBTA offers internship start dates on the first Monday of September, December, March, and June. We’re looking for candidates who are able to commit to a six-month experience, allowing for a more meaningful and impactful contribution. We are looking to hire 1 intern, who will work at least 2 - 3 days onsite (40 hours per week) for at least six months (September – February). Principal Duties and Responsibilities As an RIM Intern, you will assist the DM team in organizing, maintaining, and improving records management processes. You will gain experience in managing the document lifecycle, enhancing compliance practices, understanding the Massachusetts Statewide Records Retention Schedule and support efforts to streamline workflows. This role involves collaborating with various MBTA departments to ensure that records and data are efficiently captured, stored, and retrieved in alignment with regulatory and organizational requirements. Assist in the implementation of records management policies, procedures, and best practices.Create inventories and review those submitted by originating offices stakeholders to ensure accuracy, completeness, and compliance with retention schedules.Support the classification of physical and electronic records.Collaborate with teams across the MBTA to gather, organize, and maintain essential documentation.Participate in special projects focused on improving information governance and document management systems.Help prepare training materials and resources to promote records management awarenessParticipate in team / staff meetings, organization events, fieldwork, project teams, or other duties as needed.Additional responsibilities may include focusing on one or more departments or locations. See applicable addendums or hiring manager for department or location specific functions. Provide internal and external customers with courteous and professional experience.Perform all other duties and projects that may be assigned.* The statements contained in this job description are intended to describe the general nature and complexity of the work being performed by staff assigned to this job and do not represent an exhaustive list of all duties, tasks, and responsibilities required of staff assigned to this position. The physical demands and work environment characteristics described here-in are representative of those an employee may encounter while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations can be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform essential functions. Minimum Education Enrolled, for entire duration of employment, in the graduate / master’s degree program of an accredited college / university in a Library and Information Science and/or Archives program. Minimum Experience and Required SkillsAbove average proficiency with Microsoft applications- specifically ExcelInterest in public service, government archives, or public transportation and how it operates.At least one year of experience working responsibly with sensitive items, systems, or data.Desire to learn about or deepen experience with document control and greater document management activities in a transit authority.Ability to work independently and juggle multiple priorities.Above average ability to communicate effectively in English, both in written and oral forms.Highly organized, motivated, and have great attention-to-detail.Strong interest in public service, transit, or transportation and how it operates.Above average ability to work effectively in diverse, collaborative, fast-paced environments as well as independently.Ability to independently commute to assigned work locations in the Boston, MA metro area, as required by the role.Authorized to work in the United States (international students in CPT, STEM, or OPT programs through a university are eligible to apply). The MBTA does not provide visa sponsorship, such as H1-B status.* The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and complexity of the work being performed by staff assigned to this job and do not represent an exhaustive list of all duties, tasks, and responsibilities required.

jobs.feedName: David Nazarian College of Business & Economics
jobs.title: Fall 2026 Internship at Office of U.S. Senator Elizabeth Warren
jobs.description: Employer: Office of U.S. Senator Elizabeth Warren Expires: 07/31/2026 Senator Warren’s team is currently accepting applications for fall internships in our DC, Boston, and Springfield offices.A legislative internship in Senator Warren’s Washington DC office is designed to introduce you to almost every aspect of the office, including the legislative process, office administration, and constituent relations. Interns are not assigned full-time to a specific staff member, but rather will perform a variety of tasks. Listed below are some general tasks that legislative interns may be expected to complete: Processing daily physical mail as well as phone and digital constituent correspondence.Assisting legislative staff with projects. Conducting research and reviewing materials for our oversight and investigations team.Answering the phones and directing constituent inquiries.Attending hearings and briefings, and then writing summary memos.Giving Capitol tours to constituents and escorting them to the Capitol Visitor Center and the Senate Gallery.Hearing directly from staff about their work in our brown bag lunch series. The press internship in Senator Warren’s Washington DC office is designed to introduce participants to the office’s press and communications operation. Listed below are some tasks that press interns may be expected to complete: Processing media clips.Assisting press and communications staff with projects.Drafting communications materials including press releases, video scripts, statements, quotes, and social media content.Transcribing audio from media appearances, including interviews, floor speeches, and committee hearings.Hearing directly from staff about their work in our brown bag lunch series. The Boston and Springfield offices primarily handle constituent casework. An internship in one of our state offices will introduce you to the regional policy issues affecting our Massachusetts constituents, conducting constituent casework, engaging with government and community partners, and office administration. Interns are assigned to individual staff members to support them with casework and external outreach tasks. In addition, interns are generally responsible for the following: Sorting and processing mail.Answering the phones and directing constituent inquiries.Listening to and processing incoming voicemails.Compiling regional news reports.Assisting staffers with office projects.Hearing directly from staff about their work in our Brown Bag lunch series.Our office offers paid internships to accepted applicants who will not receive funding through their college/university or an outside program. The program is geared towards currently enrolled undergraduate students. We are currently accepting both part-time and full-time applicants. Application portals can be found on our website and the deadline for our fall program is Friday, July 10, 2026 at 11:59 pm. If you have questions during this process, please contact us at internships@warren.senate.gov.
jobs.employer: Office of U.S. Senator Elizabeth Warren

He or she will assist with the primary day-to-day account contact for clients, working to execute and manage a multitude of digital strategies. Co-worker assistance and internal team satisfaction are key areas of focus for this role. _RESPONSIBILITIES: Assist Account Team and members of the Executive team on client accountsOwn day-to-day tasks as assigned such as status documents, note taking, proposal and contract writingManage multiple projects across various businesses simultaneouslyKeep supervisor informed to status and progress of all projectsMaintain positivity and leadership in high-pressure, deadline-driven environments, with the ability to prioritize on the flyParticipate in internal and client sessions, work to develop client presentation materials and assist with writing proposals and statements of work as neededOrganizing, preparing for, and documenting notes in client meetings, following up internally to ensure client requests are completed, and other day-to-day tasks _REQUIREMENTS: Background in Marketing, Communications, Advertising, Business, or Digital Marketing fieldExcellent teamwork, organization and communication skillsExtremely proficient in Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, and Excel. PowerPoint wizardry a bonus.Proven expertise in working with teammates requiredThe ability to balance client needs with agency capabilities and properly guide the client to the ideal recommendation for their businessAbility to assist Account Managers, Supervisors, Associate Director, and Directors with direction _PAY TRANSPARENCY + INTERNSHIP BENEFITS: The pay rate for internships at Collective Measures is $20.00 per hour. Benefits available to interns include:Participation in the Employee Stock Ownership PlanCommuter stipend (if working as a hybrid employee)Participation in 401(K) Plan ABOUT COLLECTIVE MEASURES Collective Measures is the agency brands call when they’re ready to move past the noise. We don’t wait for certainty; we create it. Built at the intersection of media and analytics, we turn raw signals into directions that are clear, actionable, and built to scale. Our work fuses the logic of data with the momentum of conviction, shaping systems that turn constant change into measurable progress. We’re not theorists or tacticians; we’re builders. Every plan we design connects insight to execution, vision to proof. The result is marketing that doesn’t just perform, it propels. As an employee-owned agency, we think like stakeholders because we are. Collective Measures does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy and gender identity), national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, age, parental status, military service, or other non-merit factors.Studies have shown that women and people of color may be less likely to apply for jobs unless they meet every one of the qualifications listed. We are most interested in finding the best candidate for the job. We would encourage you to apply for a job at Collective Measures even if you don’t meet every one of our qualifications listed.

jobs.title: Service Desk Intern/Analyst at Brown Gibbons Lang & Company
jobs.description: Employer: Brown Gibbons Lang & Company Expires: 07/31/2026 Position SummaryBrown Gibbons Lang & Company (BGL) is a leading independent investment bank and financial advisory firm serving the middle market, headquartered in Cleveland with offices in Chicago and New York.The Service Desk Intern is a paid, hands-on internship within BGL’s IT team, designed for a student or early-career candidate who wants real enterprise experience in a professional, fast-paced environment. Reporting to the Service Desk Lead, the intern assists with day-to-day support tickets, device preparation, and end-user assistance while shadowing experienced analysts and building practical skills in Microsoft 365, Intune, and modern IT operations. The role emphasizes learning by doing, with responsibilities expanding as skills develop.Key ResponsibilitiesTicket assistance — Assist with logging, triaging, and resolving entry-level support tickets in FreshService under the guidance of the service desk team.Device preparation — Help prepare, image, and deploy workstations and peripherals, learning Windows Autopilot and Microsoft Intune enrollment hands-on.End-user support — Provide basic, friendly support for common hardware, software, and account questions, escalating anything beyond scope.Shadowing & learning — Shadow service desk analysts and senior IT staff to learn Microsoft 365 administration, endpoint management, and IT operations in a regulated environment.Inventory & asset support — Assist with hardware inventory, asset tagging, and equipment organization.Documentation — Contribute to knowledge base articles and how-to guides as resolutions are learned.What You’ll LearnEnterprise Microsoft 365 administration and identity basics (Entra ID).Modern endpoint management with Microsoft Intune and Windows Autopilot.Professional service desk operations, ticketing discipline, and customer service in a financial-services setting.How IT supports security and compliance in a FINRA-regulated firm.QualificationsCurrently enrolled in, or a recent graduate of, an information technology, computer science, or related program — or equivalent demonstrated technical aptitude.Working familiarity with Windows and Microsoft 365 applications.Strong customer-service instincts, curiosity, and willingness to ask questions.Reliability, professionalism, and the ability to handle confidential information appropriately.Brown Gibbons Lang & Company is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

jobs.title: Marketing and Events Intern at Breckenridge Outdoor Education Center (BOEC)
jobs.description: Employer: Breckenridge Outdoor Education Center (BOEC) Expires: 07/31/2026 Seasonal-Winter: November 1st- April 15thReports to: Marketing and Events Coordinator Position Summary: The Marketing and Events Intern supports the execution of BOEC’s fundraising and community events, as well as ongoing marketing and communications efforts. This is an opportunity to gain practical, hands-on experience in nonprofit event planning and marketing operation all while contributing to a mission-driven organization in a beautiful mountain setting.This role is ideal for a college student, recent graduate, or early-career professional who is eager to learn, takes initiative, and is excited to be part of a collaborative and purpose-filled team. Responsibilities:Event SupportAssist the Marketing and Events Coordinator with planning and preparation for major annual events hosted by BOEC. Assist with other events including community engagement events, beneficiary events, and staff events.Support logistics coordination including organizing materials, managing timelines, designing signage, and tracking donations or auction items.Provide on-site assistance during events including setup, registration, greeting guests, and working with volunteers.Help gather and organize event-related data, feedback, and photos.Marketing SupportDesign promotional materials, flyers, and graphic content for events and programs in Canva or InDesign.Represent BOEC professionally and passionately at events, programs, and community functions.Maintain and update website content with relevant stories and events.Support BOEC social media presence through content capture, testimonial capture, editing, and posting.Support BOEC email communications efforts through writing and editing. Other projects as assigned by the Marketing and Events Coordinator. Schedule:5 days a week, 9 AM - 5 PM at the BOEC Admin office in Breckenridge, with flexibility for activities and events.May occasionally work weekends or evenings based events. Benefits:Private Room in BOEC Staff Shared Housing$500 Monthly StipendParticipation in BOEC EAP/Building Hope Mental Health ResourcesAccess to BOEC outdoor gear with approvalPro purchase accessParking spot at BOEC Admin Building(Winter Internship Only) Epic Local Ski Pass (Keystone and Breckenridge) and opportunities to ski at Copper Mountain and Arapahoe Basin RequirementsBe at least 21 years of age. Have a valid drivers license.Be willing to participate and pass a background/MVR check. A strong interest in event planning, nonprofit marketing, and/or adaptive outdoor recreation.Progression toward or completion of event management, marketing, communications, advertising or related degree.Administrative work, events, media, and/or marketing experience preferred.Organized and detail-oriented with a willingness to learn and take direction.Creative thinker with strong writing, editing, and content creation skills.Positive attitude and flexibility in a fast-paced, mission-driven environment.Strong interpersonal and communication skills; ability to work independently and as part of a team.Applied understanding of basic marketing principles, familiarity with social media platforms, basic graphic design skills.Interest in outdoor recreation and comfort in outdoor settings is a must. About BOEC: The Breckenridge Outdoor Education Center (BOEC) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to transforming lives through outdoor experiences for people with disabilities and additional needs. Since 1976, we’ve provided high-quality, inclusive outdoor adventures in skiing, snowboarding, rafting, cycling, climbing, and more for individuals of all abilities. With the help of a dedicated staff and 300+ volunteers, we serve over 2,500 participants annually through our Adaptive Ski & Snowboard Program, Wilderness Program, and Internship Program.

jobs.feedName: David Nazarian College of Business & Economics
jobs.title: Videography Intern at Namify
jobs.description: Employer: Namify Expires: 07/31/2026 ABOUT US:Namify is in the business of belonging. For over 20 years, companies of all sizes have partnered with us to elevate their recognition programs, corporate gifting strategies, branding initiatives, and employee engagement. Through automation and more sustainable practices, we are revolutionizing an industry that has traditionally been wasteful and awkward. We are looking for energetic “inclusion superheroes” to join the cause. If you are a detail-oriented person and like the idea of creating more happiness in the workplace, we want to talk to you … like yesterday. We’re growing fast because the world recognizes how desperately services like ours are needed.ABOUT THIS JOB: The Videography Intern will support the creation of high-quality video content that showcases Namify's products, services, and swag management software, AXOMO. This temporary internship will run through the end of October and provides hands-on experience in all aspects of video production, including planning, filming, editing, and post-production. The intern will primarily work from Namify's office, with occasional opportunities to travel to capture company events and customer experiences. This role is ideal for someone looking to build their videography portfolio while gaining real-world marketing and brand storytelling experience. RESPONSIBILITIES:Produce and coordinate various videos and digital content that promote our products and, services and brand Assist in the creation and maintenance of up-to-date training videos for internal useCollaborate with team members to effectively place and advertise these creative worksAssist with the execution of webinars and other online conferencesSupport our internal branding, culture initiatives, and recruiting by capturing and promoting compelling video and photosAssist with occasional media creation for larger clientsAll other duties as assigned.BENEFITS AND COMPENSATION:$19/hourMonthly company lunchesExperience daysSCHEDULEMonday - Friday, 9:00am-5:00pm - In Office Remote work is not available RequirementsMust have proper documentation for employment in the United States Must pass a pre-employment drug screenREQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, EXPERIENCE, AND ABILITIES:One or more years of film experience preferredBasic knowledge of Adobe Creative Suite -Including Premiere Pro, and After EffectsExperience planning, filming, and editing video content for marketing, social media, training, or similar projectsAbility to understand and connect with various audiencesBasic script writing, storytelling, and creative writing skillsMust be able to work in either a standing or sitting position for 6-8 hours a day.Must be able to lift 30 pounds.A collaborative attitude and willingness to be flexible and accommodating in a fast-paced environmentAbility to manage multiple deadlines and quotasStrong work ethic, desire to learn and growMust be able to communicate effectively with team members at all levels.Must have great attention to detail, even with repetitive processes.Must be able to follow instructions, both written and verbal.Perform quality work within deadlines with and without direct supervision.Interact professionally with other employees, customers and suppliers.Work effectively as a team contributor on all assignments.Work independently while understanding the necessity for communicating and coordinating work efforts with other employees and organizations. SPECIAL CONSIDERATION FOR: Previous podcast, webinar experience or live streaming experience PERFORMANCE METRICS:On-time completion of projectsProduce engaging, high-quality video content that supports brand awareness, and client communication goals Complete assigned video projects on schedule, including and average of at least four edited videos per month (including at least one Axomo promotional video) THIS POSITION REPORTS TO: Brand Manager WORK SPACE INFO: This job primarily operates in an office environment. Periodically, the videographer conducts business with the production team in a warehouse environment which requires standing, moving, and lifting.TRAINING:Training for this position will be completed by the department manager. Training will consist of education on key processes, preparation and staging requirements by customer, location of tools, supplies and materials. You will be trained on how to use the order interface, how to properly read orders and where to look for notes. Additional training will be completed as processes evolve or as required.SAFETY:Namify enforces a safety culture whereby all employees have the responsibility for continuously developing and maintaining a safe working environment. Each incumbent is responsible for completing all training requirements and fulfilling self-aid/buddy-aid responsibilities, participating in emergency response tasks as requested, and serving on safety committees and teams as requested. In addition, the incumbent must accept that they have responsibility for maintaining the safety of themselves and others by adhering to all written and verbal instructions, promptly reporting and/or correcting all hazards or unsafe conditions, questioning nonstandard operations or environmental factors that may involve unmitigated hazards, and providing feedback to site leads and management on all safety issues.

jobs.description: Employer: Northwestern Mutual - Orange County Expires: 07/31/2026 Our College Financial Representative program is recognized as a top internship program by:WayUP Top Internship Program in 2025, 2024 and 20232025 Forbes’ “Best Place to Work for New Grads” AwardAPEX Training Award in 202525 consecutive years awarded Vault Top Internship Program The program trains interns in the areas of entrepreneurship, sales, business development, marketing, and communication. jobs.feedName: David Nazarian College of Business & Economics
jobs.title: Luxury Travel & Guest Experience Intern at Greek Concierge
jobs.description: Employer: Greek Concierge Expires: 07/31/2026 Greek Concierge is a private concierge house attending to a considered circle of travellers in Greece — its islands, its coast, its quiet interior. We are opening a paid internship for a student of hospitality, travel or the liberal arts who wants to learn how bespoke travel is truly made. Part-time during term or full-time for a summer cohort - 10–12 weeks / one semester, with a named concierge as your mentor - Paid — a monthly stipend, stated with this posting - On completion, a portfolio of your published research and itineraries, and formal verification of hours where your programme requires it

We partner with growing businesses to streamline hiring, reduce time-to-fill, and deliver flexible workforce solutions aligned with real business needs.As we expand our U.S. presence, we’re building a high-performance sales team with clear progression into leadership. This role is designed for someone ambitious, coachable, and motivated to grow into a regional sales leader. Role OverviewThe Sales Intern will be responsible for generating new business opportunities, engaging prospective clients, and building a strong sales pipeline in the U.S. market.This is a high-activity, performance-driven role focused on outbound prospecting, inbound lead qualification, and relationship development. You will also represent Talentpoel at industry events and networking opportunities in the Atlanta market.You will often be the first point of contact for potential clients, making your communication skills, professionalism, and ability to build trust critical to success.This position is structured with a clear pathway to becoming a Sales Lead for the Northeast U.S. Key ResponsibilitiesConduct outbound prospecting via email, LinkedIn, and phoneQualify inbound leads and assess client hiring needsBook discovery meetings and support deal progressionClose early-stage deals during probation and growth phasesAttend industry events and represent Talentpoel professionallyBuild and maintain an organized pipeline within the CRMResearch target companies and key decision-makersDevelop personalized outreach campaigns to improve response ratesConsistently follow up with prospects to move them through the sales funnelTrack KPIs, conversion metrics, and revenue performanceCollaborate with marketing and delivery teams to align messaging and targeting Ideal CandidateStrong written and verbal communication skillsComfortable with high-volume outreach and performance targetsHighly organized, proactive, and results-drivenCoachable and motivated to grow into a leadership roleInterest in recruitment, talent solutions, or HR technology is a plus What Success Looks LikeConsistent outbound activity and pipeline growthStrong meeting conversion ratesClosed deals during probation and growth stagesPositive prospect feedback on initial interactionsMeeting and exceeding defined KPIsDemonstrated leadership potential and ownership mindset Compensation & Growth PathWe offer a structured, performance-based progression model with clear milestones and mentorship at every stage. Phase 1: Probationary Training Period (6 months) 8 hours per week (Max) | $12/hour + CommissionThis phase is focused on training, mentorship, and skill development.During this stage, you will:Receive hands-on sales training and direct mentorshipLearn our ICP, positioning, outreach strategy, and CRM systemsBegin outbound prospecting and pipeline developmentRepresent Talentpoel at events and networking engagementsWork toward clearly defined activity and performance KPIsYou are eligible for commission on all deals closed during this stage.Progression requires meeting defined KPIs and demonstrating the ability to contribute to revenue generation. Phase 2: Growth Stage (Part-Time)20 hours per week | $2,000/month + CommissionAfter meeting your KPIs and successfully closing 10 deals, you will transition into the Growth Stage.In this phase, you will:Take greater ownership of pipeline and revenue targetsManage client conversations more independentlyContribute consistently to monthly revenue goalsCommission continues to apply to all closed deals. Phase 3: Full-Time Leadership TrackFull-Time | $60,000 Base + Commission (On-Target Earnings: $90,000 – $115,000)After demonstrating sustained revenue performance, consistently meeting KPIs, and making a measurable strategic impact during the Growth Stage, you will transition into a full-time role.This compensation structure is performance-driven, with on-target earnings ranging from $90K–$115K based on achieving revenue goals.This role is designed to evolve into a Sales Lead position for the Northeast U.S., with responsibility for regional growth, revenue ownership, and sales strategy execution. What We OfferPerformance-based commission structure from Day 1Clear, merit-based progression into leadershipFlexible remote/hybrid working modelDirect mentorship and hands-on trainingHigh-growth environment with real ownershipThe opportunity to help build Talentpoel’s U.S. expansion from the ground up

jobs.feedName: David Nazarian College of Business & Economics
jobs.title: Fall 2026 Program and Communications Intern at US-China Education Trust
jobs.description: Employer: US-China Education Trust Expires: 08/01/2026 Program and Communications Intern Application Deadline: Rolling applications accepted through July 21. Time commitment: Interns are expected to work 20+ hours per week Compensation: Interns may elect to receive academic credit per their university’s policies or a one-time stipend paid upon internship completion. Organization Description:The US-China Education Trust (USCET), a program of the F.Y. Chang Foundation, is a 501(c) (3) non-profit organization that promotes U.S.-China relations by strengthening mutual understanding through education and exchange. For more than 20 years, USCET has strengthened links between Chinese and American universities, scholars, and policymakers, while its programs provide a nuanced understanding of the U.S.-China relationship, American society, and Asian American achievements within the larger context of the U.S.-China relationship. Position Description: USCET seeks self-motivated, highly organized, and tech-savvy program and communications interns to provide programmatic and communication support to the organization’s program staff and work as part of a dynamic team. They will play key roles in supporting the implementation of ongoing programs as well as developing new initiatives addressing critical issues related to U.S.-China relations. Interns are responsible for creating meaningful content for USCET’s social media presence and connecting with relevant audiences. Responsibilities:1. Communications and Outreach Facilitate online communications - Write updates and announcements for the USCET website, newsletters and promotional materials, and other website-content; Support social media communications - Update USCET social media platforms, including LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, and other platformsDraft remarks and talking points for USCET’s Executive chair and staffCreate dynamic written, graphic and video content, including invitations, brochures, and other materials for programs and events Pitch strategies to increase USCET’s social media and website engagement; 2. Reporting, grant writing, and research - Contribute to the design and development of new initiatives: Conduct research to inform the development of new initiatives related to U.S.-China relations and the Asian American experience, as well as on new development prospects; Write concept notes and develop grant proposals; • Draft reports for internal records and for the Advisory Council and donors, including individuals, foundations, and government agencies. Qualifications: Be a current undergraduate juniors, seniors, and graduate student. Show demonstrated academic and personal interests in international relations, diplomacy, and U.S.-China relations. Exhibit excellent communication, writing and editing skills. Display strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to multitask and carry out tasks independently. Exhibit strong computer skills, including proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Constant Contact, WordPress, Canva, CRM Software, and other web based tools. Graphic design and web development skills. Be proficient in Chinese language. Compensation:Interns may elect to receive academic credit per their university’s policies, or a one-time stipend ($1,500 for 20+ hours per week) paid upon internship completion. To be eligible for the stipend, interns must have satisfactory work performance and commit to a minimum of 20 hours per week for fifteen weeks. Our internship program is open to US citizens, permanent residents, students, and recent graduates who are eligible to work in the United States. We are unable to sponsor visas. How to apply: Send cover letter, resume, and two short writing samples of no more than three pages, single-spaced each to employment@uscet.org. Location: Our office is at the Elliott School of International Affairs, George Washington University 1957 E Street NW, Washington DC. We maintain a hybrid schedule, so fully remote interns will not be considered.

jobs.feedName: David Nazarian College of Business & Economics
jobs.title: NDRC Fall Internships at National Democratic Redistricting Committee
jobs.description: Employer: National Democratic Redistricting Committee Expires: 08/01/2026 Thank you for your interest in the National Redistricting Action Fund (NRAF). NRAF is the 501(c)(4) affiliate of the National Democratic Redistricting Committee (NDRC). The mission of NRAF is to harness the power of people and the courts to dismantle unfair electoral maps and create a redistricting system based on democratic values. By helping to create more just and representative electoral districts across the country, we also hope to restore the public’s faith in a true representative democracy. NRAF offers paid internship opportunities during Spring, Summer, and Fall, for current undergraduate and graduate students only. Our internships are designed to be an integral part of your educational experience, offering practical skills and insights that complement your academic studies.We accept applications on a rolling basis, and currently have all applications open for Summer and Fall 2026. If you applied for our internship program for any of our 2025 cohorts or earlier, please reapply to be considered for the remaining 2026 cohorts. Summer internships are full time (up to 40 hours per week) and Fall internships are part time (up to 20 hours per week). NRAF offers internships in six departments*. You can find the job description for each internship below. Brand, Creative, and SocialCampaignsCommunicationsFinanceMappingMobilization*Are you a JD/MPP/MPA candidate looking for the National Redistricting Foundation’s legal and policy internships? Click here. Please note at this time, legal and policy internships are available through NRAF’s affiliated 501(c)(3) organization, the NRF, and are only open to current JD, MPP, and MPA candidates. Technical ConsiderationsThis role requires access to a computer or laptop that can operate Google Suite seamlessly.Applicant familiarity with Google Suite (GMail, GCal, GDocs, Sheets, Etc.) is preferred. To learn more about using Google Suite, visit the Google Workspace Learning Center. Important 2026 DatesFall 2026Begins: Monday, September 21Ends: Friday, November 20Applications close: Sunday, July 26Tentative interview period: Monday, August 3 through Friday, August 21* *Due to the high volume of applications we receive, we are unable to provide status updates to every applicant, however we guarantee you will be notified of any decisions once applications close for that semester. NRAF is proud to be an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an environment that celebrates diversity, equity, and inclusion. People of color, trans and gender non-conforming individuals, women, people with differing abilities, and veterans are strongly encouraged to apply. Questions? Reach out to jobs@redistrict.org.

jobs.feedName: David Nazarian College of Business & Economics
jobs.title: Construction Co-Op Warren NJ - Summer 2027 at Higley Construction
jobs.description: Employer: Higley Construction Expires: 08/01/2026 About the Company – Celebrating 100 Years of Building Community & 100% Employee-Owned Higley Construction has been one of the most experienced and respected construction firms in the region. The company surpassed 100 years in business—a century shaped by an unwavering commitment to its core values of people, ethics, respect, commitment, and excellence. Since its founding in 1925, Higley Construction has partnered with clients to bring their goals and visions to life, consistently guided by honesty, integrity, and mutual respect. At Higley Construction, we build more than buildings — we build trust, relationships, and communities. From the offices where you work, to the schools where you learn, and the hospitals where you heal, we create environments where people can live, work, and thrive. With every project, we go beyond bricks and mortar to connect people and place, delivering spaces that stand the test of time. Position Overview:The Operations Co-Op is designed to introduce college students to office and field experiences in the commercial construction profession. The Co-Op rotation exposes the student to a wide array of professional interactions among management, project managers, field workers, consulting professionals, clients, and related trades, exploring the role of each within a project. The Co-Op will work under corporate managers, project managers, or field superintendents on a variety of construction tasks. Ultimately, this program intends to enhance the Co-Op’s individual abilities by gaining on-site knowledge of how a project idea becomes a construction reality.The Co-Op will either be assigned to an active construction project site and/or a construction specialized department located in Warren, New Jersey for the Summer of 2027!Responsibilities:Learn and understand project design drawings and specifications to accurately interpret and gather necessary information. Capture and document project site photographs to record construction progress and existing conditions before starting work in new areas.Acquire knowledge of the submittal process for obtaining approval of building materials.Prepare and effectively maintain a submittal log.Assist the Superintendent and Project Manager in preparing and processing along with maintaining Request for Information (RFI) logs for tracking purposes.Collaborate with the Project Manager and Superintendent to prepare weekly meeting minutes for subcontractor and Owner-Architect-Contractor (OAC) meetings.Prepare daily reports summarizing project activities.Attend safety meetings, walkthroughs, and weekly toolbox talks.Review weekly safety audits with the Superintendent to ensure compliance and address any issues.Participate in quality meetings and quality walkthroughs to ensure project standards are met.Adhere strictly to safety procedures, includes personal protection apparel and equipment.Software’s Utilized: Bluebeam, Microsoft Office, Flashtract, Plangrid, Microsoft SharePoint, Jonas Premier, HammerTech (Safety/Quality), Oracle P6 (Project Controls), Onscreen-Takeoff (Preconstruction).Performs other related duties as assigned.Skills and Abilities:Strong competency in business writing and public presentation skills, with a drive to become a compelling presenter, motivator, and team leader. Demonstrates exceptional personal work habits and a forward-thinking approach.Proficient in, or eager to learn, various construction software applications.Exhibits proactive, curious, and innovative thinking, with a strong desire to learn and contribute.Ability to work independently, with direction and supervision, and in a team environment.Excellent mathematical, problem-solving, and relationship management skills.Skilled in assessing and solving problems with a strategic mindset.Qualifications: Enrolled and actively pursuing a college or university accredited degree program in civil engineering, construction management, or a related architectural/engineering field.Desire to work in construction management and to learn about the construction industry.

Gantt Center for African- American Arts + Culture Fall/Spring 2026 EnVision Me Internship Internship: Semester Long (Fall: August 20 - December 19) (Spring: December - May) Compensation: Stipend $1500, School Credit Application Start: Fall 2026 Internship: August 15 Spring 2027: December 1Learn more about the Envision Me Program here Envision Me Program OverviewEnVision Me is a holistic educational program that empowers high school students to engage with digital media arts for self-expression and personal development, while gaining skills for future success. By combining critical thinking, artistic skills, and technology, EnVision Me equips students with the tools to tell their stories, collaborate effectively, and succeed academically — all while nurturing their creativity and self-confidence.Internship Description EnVision Me is seeking motivated and creative college sophomores, juniors, and seniors studying digital media, film, or photography to join our internship program. Interns will serve as mentors and creative guides, working directly alongside high school students to support their growth as visual storytellers and help them bring their "Into My Lens" documentary projects to life.This paid internship offers a meaningful opportunity to apply your academic training in a real-world, community-centered setting while making a lasting impact on the next generation of digital media artists. Intern Responsibilities Assist students with technology equipment (iMacs, mics, and Adobe Creative Cloud functionalities)Assist the teacher with teaching new concepts and attend weekly sessions to assist program participants one - on - one with video editing, filming, storyboarding, and voiceovers during weekly sessions at the partner school. Research and compile information and activities, experiences for educational materials for Envision Me lessonsPhotograph/videotape programs in progress and forward them to the program coordinator Review and organize EnVision Me equipment and suppliesAttend Envision Me debrief meeting at the end of the program semester; 1 or 2 class field trips, and end of the semester showcaseComplete 1 weekly meeting with Envision Me staff to review sessions virtually or in-personHoursWeekly hours range from 5 - 8 hours (7-8 hours is max) Stipend will be divided into two amounts of $750 after completing required # of sessions of your internship. QualificationsKnowledge and passion for digital media arts (film, photography, audiography)Has experience with Adobe Lightroom Classic, Adobe Premiere Pro, Adobe Audition, and Adobe After EffectsCurrent Undergraduate Student pursuing in Film, Photography, Digital Media Arts, or Mass Communications Degree Creative, Energetic, Outgoing, Helpful, and OrganizedOutstanding photography and or cinematography skillsAbility to teach and guide students with equipment and Adobe Creative CloudExcellent Communication SkillsExperience with working with students and multi-taskingProfessional and head-forward attitudeAbility to lift +30lbs of equipment How To ApplyPlease send the items below to Nicole Beverly, nicole@ganttcenter.org:Your ResumeYour Portfolio or a link to access your film(s) and photography skills Professional or University Recommendation Letter Questions?For any additional questions, please emailNicole Beverly: nicole@ganttcenter.org

jobs.feedName: David Nazarian College of Business & Economics
jobs.title: Video Production & Social Media Marketing Intern at Skyline Nav AI, Inc.
jobs.description: Employer: Skyline Nav AI, Inc. Expires: 08/01/2026 We are seeking a highly creative, self-motivated, and execution-oriented Video Production & Social Media Marketing Intern to join our growth team starting August 1st. This is not a traditional "shadowing" internship; you will own an entire multimedia project from scratch. Your primary mission will be to conceptualize, record, edit, and launch a brand-new, high-impact video campaign series designed to tell compelling stories about cutting-edge technology and drive commercial interest in our advanced drone solutions. If you are a builder at heart who loves capturing the passion of technology, turning technical innovations into engaging visual narratives, and scaling digital communities, this role is for you. Key ResponsibilitiesEnd-to-End Video Production: Own the full video lifecycle from scratch. This includes scripting, storyboarding, setting up equipment, capturing high-quality footage, interviewing team members, audio mixing, color grading, and final editing.Campaign Development: Design and execute a stylized, episodic video series structured around themes of technological innovation, deep-tech engineering, and the real-world impact of advanced hardware.Social Media & Content Strategy: Package and distribute video assets into multi-platform formats (long-form for YouTube/LinkedIn, short-form snippets for LinkedIn/X/YouTube/Instagram/) to maximize organic reach and engagement.Growth Marketing: Align visual storytelling with commercial metrics, creating content that increases brand awareness, educates target audiences, and ultimately accelerates lead generation and market adoption for our autonomous drone systems.Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work directly with our world-class engineering, product, and leadership teams to translate highly technical milestones into accessible, inspiring visual media. Qualifications & SkillsTechnical Portfolio: Proven experience in video editing and production, with a strong portfolio showcasing multi-platform storytelling, smooth editing pacing, and crisp audio/visual quality.Software Proficiency: Advanced skills in professional editing suites such as Canva, Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, DaVinci Resolve, and Adobe After Effects.Marketing Savvy: Solid understanding of social media growth frameworks, algorithm dynamics, and optimal formatting across LinkedIn, YouTube, and other modern channels.Interest in Deep Tech: A genuine passion for robotics, aerospace, autonomous systems, drones, or artificial intelligence.Autonomy & Drive: Exceptional project management skills; ability to take a high-level creative vision and execute it independently with tight deadlines. What We OfferDirect, hands-on ownership of an extensive digital campaign that will prominently feature in your portfolio.Mentorship from senior leadership and technical pioneers in the defense-tech and AI landscapes.A fast-paced, highly innovative startup environment where your creative work directly impacts company growth and market reach.

jobs.feedName: David Nazarian College of Business & Economics
jobs.title: 2027 Summer Internship at Walter Shuffain
jobs.description: Employer: Walter Shuffain Expires: 08/01/2026 Walter Shuffain - 2027 Summer InternshipAn internship at Walter Shuffain provides a well-rounded experience in our accounting, auditing, and tax departments. Our interns quickly become valuable team members and many have opted to join the firm after graduation. Interns have an opportunity to work on a variety of client engagements under the supervision of senior firm personnel. These engagements include: assisting with tax compliance and reporting through tax strategy development and return preparation; providing accounting and financial reporting through audit, review, and compilation services.Interns are introduced to the firm's software programs and may assist with monitoring clients' industry information online, as well as performing online research.But it's not all work at Walter Shuffain. We’re a fairly fun bunch of accountants and our interns join us for a summer and intern outings. Essential Functions:Become familiar with firm policies and procedures.Understand the rules, requirements, and code of conduct of the AICPA, and the authoritative literature established by the FASB and GASB.Obtain a working knowledge of tax and accounting research techniques and the various research sources of the firm’s library.Obtain a working knowledge of firm technology, including the use of e-mail, the Internet, spreadsheets and tax software.Become proficient in the preparation of corporate, individual, partnership, fiduciary and other tax forms.Assume responsibility for completion of assignments.Perform assigned audit, review and compilation procedures under the supervision of more experienced staff.Develop good work habits.Learn to work effectively with co-workers and supervisors.Get involved in some structured FUN activities!Training:In addition to our week long initial training and on the job training, the candidate will receive computer training with our IT Manager as well as ongoing use of firm related software.The program includes many fun activities, including summer outing, community service, intern outings, and many more!! Plus we have Fridays off in the summer and casual dress!!Those interested in applying for an internship position should submit resume through handshake.Learn more about how you can create your own professional path to success: https://www.wscpa.com/careers/
